content.text:
Looking at the image, the track ahead is a long straight running eastward with the car well-centered and aligned, heading nearly parallel to the track direction.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 114, target = (114 + 5) % 120 = 119
- Waypoint 119: x=5.9053, y=0.6836
- Car: x=2.3293, y=0.7638
- Bearing = atan2(0.6836 - 0.7638, 5.9053 - 2.3293) = atan2(-0.0802, 3.576) = -1.284°
- Current heading: -1.63°
- Delta = -1.284° - (-1.63°) = +0.346° → essentially straight, no correction needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Long straight ahead, car heading -1.63° nearly matches track bearing -1.28°, delta is only +0.35° so go straight at full speed.",
  "knowledge": [
    "After waypoint 108, track runs nearly due east at y≈0.68 — long straight ahead",
    "Car heading has recovered to ~-1.6°, nearly aligned with track direction",
    "Positive delta = steer left (positive steering_angle), negative = steer right",
    "At delta < 5°, use steering_angle=0 at speed 1.5 to avoid oscillation",
    "Car is slightly left of center (~0.09m) — within safe bounds at track width 0.76m",
    "Waypoints 114-119 all at y≈0.68, x increasing — long eastward straight",
    "Oversteer warning: once delta drops below 5°, use steering_angle=0 immediately",
    "Car heading fully recovered to ~-1.6° — no more left steering needed on straight",
    "Progress at 95.9% — approaching end of lap, maintain alignment for clean finish",
    "Track curves right (south) around waypoint 24-30 based on waypoint data"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [2.057, 2.135, 2.238, 2.329],
    "y": [0.735, 0.734, 0.743, 0.764],
    "yaw": [-24.3, -17.3, -8.8, -1.6]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 0.35,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 0.0
}
```
